+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-89
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-22_13-53-57+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs — added one new regression test inside the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block, parallel to `r_key_reload_on_missing_file_preserves_prior_frame`.
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-89_TASK_2026-05-22_13-41-29+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs`: no production code touched. The only edit is the addition of one new `#[test]` function inside the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block, between the existing `r_key_reload_on_missing_file_preserves_prior_frame` test and the ISSUE-85 watcher section banner (insertion at line 1903 of the prior file; diff is 179 insertions, 0 deletions).
+
+### Tests
+
+- `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs::tests::r_key_reload_on_malformed_glb_retains_then_recovers`: new regression test that pins the parser-failure reload path against an existing GLB file whose contents are malformed. Three legs, mirroring the structure of the canonical missing-file test:
+  1. Stage a temp file at `std::env::temp_dir()/rge-editor-issue89-<pid>-<nanos>/malformed.glb` containing exactly `b"not a glb"` (wrong-magic bytes). Directly assert `GlbLoaderHook.reload_glb(&malformed_path).is_err()` BEFORE any GPU-dependent setup, so the parser-failure proof runs even on no-GPU CI.
+  2. Build a shell from `cube.glb` via `build_shell_from_fixture("cube.glb")`, render frame 1 via `render_shell_one_frame`, and assert the central-row signature has `cube_pixel_count > 8` and is blue-dominant (`avg_b > avg_g > avg_r`). Attach the malformed path through `shell.attach_glb_reload_source(malformed_path.clone(), GlbLoaderHook)`, drive `shell.handle_asset_reload()`, render frame 2. Frame 2 assertions: `cube_pixel_count > 8`; still blue-dominant; and per-channel `(|avg_r_diff|, |avg_g_diff|, |avg_b_diff|) <= CUBE_THRESHOLD` against frame 1.
+  3. Overwrite the SAME temp path with valid bytes read from the existing `textured_uv_cube.glb` fixture (skip leg if fixture missing). Call `shell.handle_asset_reload()` again through the same already-attached source — proves the failed parse did not poison the hook/source path. Render frame 3 and assert `cube_pixel_count > 8`, `red_spread > 50 || blue_spread > 50` (textured_uv_cube checkerboard variance), and at least one channel of avg RGB shifts by `> CUBE_THRESHOLD` against frame 1/2 — proves the swap actually landed.
+  - Best-effort cleanup of the temp file + temp dir at the end of the success path.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None changed.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+`editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` — one new `#[test]` function added in the existing test module, placed adjacent to the existing R-key reload tests so the grouping reads as `r_key_reload_swaps_to_textured_uv_cube_end_to_end` → `r_key_reload_on_missing_file_preserves_prior_frame` → `r_key_reload_on_malformed_glb_retains_then_recovers`. No other lines changed in the file; the diff is purely additive.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+All gates enumerated in the Task Packet ran to completion locally:
+
+- `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` → exit 0 (after one initial fmt pass on the newly added test; final `--check` is clean).
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor --bin rge-editor r_key_reload_on_malformed -- --nocapture` → exit 0; 1 passed (`tests::r_key_reload_on_malformed_glb_retains_then_recovers`); 0 failed; 0 ignored; 61 filtered out.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor --bin rge-editor r_key_reload -- --nocapture` → exit 0; 3 passed (`r_key_reload_on_missing_file_preserves_prior_frame`, `r_key_reload_swaps_to_textured_uv_cube_end_to_end`, `r_key_reload_on_malformed_glb_retains_then_recovers`); 0 failed.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor --bin rge-editor` → exit 0; 62 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored.
+- `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` → exit 0; "VERIFY OK: all 6 verification step(s) passed." (fmt → architecture lints → architecture-lint test suite → cargo deny → cargo test --workspace → cargo test --workspace --doc).
+- `git status --short` → only `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` modified (plus the `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-89_TASK_*.md`/`.meta.json` Planner inputs that pre-existed at session start, and this newly written `ISSUE-89_EXEC_*.md` execution report). No other tracked files dirty; no new untracked files outside the allowed packet.
+
+Local environment is a headless workstation with a working GPU adapter — frame 1/2/3 readback assertions all ran (no `SKIP: no GPU adapter` paths were exercised this run). The direct `GlbLoaderHook.reload_glb(&malformed_path).is_err()` assertion also runs ahead of the GPU-dependent path, so a no-GPU CI machine would still get parser-failure proof from this test.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope.
+
+- Source changes are limited to `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` inside `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (per `MAY edit`).
+- Exactly one new `#[test]` was added (`r_key_reload_on_malformed_glb_retains_then_recovers`); it is content-malformed coverage, not another missing-file test.
+- The malformed file is created on disk with `b"not a glb"` before any reload is exercised; parser failure is directly asserted via `GlbLoaderHook.reload_glb(&path).is_err()` before driving the shell.
+- No production code, watcher code, importer code, render code, fixtures, `Cargo.{toml,lock}`, schemas, scripts, docs, ADRs, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `change.md`, or any other out-of-scope file was touched.
+- No new fixture / source / dep / packet was added beyond this required execution report.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- None — the test name `r_key_reload_on_malformed_glb_retains_then_recovers` was chosen to (a) match the verification gate's `r_key_reload_on_malformed` substring filter, (b) start with the `r_key_reload` prefix used by the other R-key reload tests so the broader gate continues to pick it up, and (c) describe both invariants (retention on failure + recovery on overwrite) without overflowing rustfmt's name-length comfort. Flag if a shorter or differently scoped name is preferred.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` modified (179 insertions, 0 deletions; test-only).
+- Untracked items: `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-89_TASK_2026-05-22_13-41-29+0300.md` and its `.meta.json` (orchestrator/Planner inputs, pre-existing at session start); `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-89_EXEC_2026-05-22_13-53-57+0300.md` (this execution report).
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-89`
+- Last commit: `fcb764e` chore(dispatch): tighten task #3 with non-poison MUST + malformed variant pin
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-89
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

@@ -0,0 +1,109 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-89
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-22_13-41-29+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-89_EXEC_*.md
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Add one test-only `rge-editor::tests` regression for GitHub issue #89 that covers the parser-failure reload path for an existing GLB file whose contents are malformed. The new coverage must be parallel to `r_key_reload_on_missing_file_preserves_prior_frame`, but distinct from the missing-file path: use wrong-magic bytes such as `b"not a glb"`, prove the malformed reload returns `Err`, prove the prior rendered frame is retained, then overwrite the same path with valid GLB bytes and prove a later reload still succeeds and changes the rendered pixel signature.
+
+Required invariants from the issue:
+
+- MUST retain the prior rendered frame after the malformed reload
+- MUST follow the failed reload with a valid reload that proves the hook still works
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs`, only inside the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block. Keep edits to one new regression test plus small test-local helper code only if the existing helpers are not enough.
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-89_EXEC_*.md`, only the execution report required by the handoff protocol.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/asset_reload.rs`, including `EditorShell::handle_asset_reload`.
+- `editor/rge-editor/src/glb_watcher.rs` or any watcher, debounce, notify, or redraw-drain code.
+- Any production code outside the existing `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` test module.
+- Any shader, render pipeline, GPU upload, material, mesh, GLB importer/exporter, asset-store, kernel, or architecture-lint code.
+- `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, workspace dependency declarations, feature flags, CI/workflow files, scripts, schemas, `.gitignore`, docs, ADRs, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `change.md`, or any packet other than the required `ISSUE-89_EXEC_*.md`.
+- Existing GLB fixtures or binary assets.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-89_EXEC_*.md`, only as the required execution report.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New source files, new test files, new crates, new fixtures, new screenshots, new golden images, new docs, new scripts, new schemas, new lint files, new CI/config files, or any handoff packet except the required execution report.
+- Runtime temporary files are allowed inside the test via `std::env::temp_dir()` and should be cleaned up best-effort; they must not become tracked files.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- One new `#[test]` in `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` under the existing `rge-editor::tests` module, placed near the existing R-key reload tests and parallel in shape to `r_key_reload_on_missing_file_preserves_prior_frame`.
+- The test must use an existing valid fixture for the initial render, preferably `cube.glb`, then write a temporary `.glb` path containing wrong-magic bytes exactly `b"not a glb"` or an equivalent wrong-magic byte string.
+- The test must directly assert that `GlbLoaderHook.reload_glb(&malformed_path)` returns `Err` for the wrong-magic bytes. Do not infer the parser failure solely from unchanged pixels or logs.
+- The test must attach the same temporary path to the shell, call `shell.handle_asset_reload()`, render frame 2, and assert frame 2 matches frame 1 within `CUBE_THRESHOLD`.
+- The test must overwrite the same temporary path with valid GLB bytes from an existing fixture, preferably `textured_uv_cube.glb`, call `shell.handle_asset_reload()` again through the same attached reload source, and assert the valid reload succeeds and changes the rendered pixel signature.
+- An `ISSUE-89_EXEC_*.md` execution report documenting changed files, the exact assertions added, verification results, and whether any headless-GPU-dependent pixel assertions skipped due to no local adapter.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The diff is test-only: source changes are limited to `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` inside `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`, plus the execution report packet.
+- Exactly one new regression test is added for this issue. It is content-malformed GLB coverage, not another missing-file test.
+- The malformed file exists on disk and contains wrong GLB magic bytes such as `b"not a glb"` before the malformed reload is exercised.
+- The test asserts the malformed parser-failure path returns `Err`, preferably by directly invoking `GlbLoaderHook.reload_glb(&malformed_path)` before driving `shell.handle_asset_reload()`.
+- Frame 1 is rendered from a known valid starting fixture and has a meaningful on-cube central-row signature, including `cube_pixel_count > 8`.
+- After `shell.handle_asset_reload()` reads the malformed bytes, frame 2 is rendered from the same shell and has a meaningful on-cube central-row signature. Frame 2 must match frame 1 within `CUBE_THRESHOLD`; an acceptable implementation is asserting the absolute deltas of the central-row average RGB channels are each `<= CUBE_THRESHOLD`.
+- The malformed reload must also preserve the prior qualitative signature, for example the `cube.glb` blue-dominant ordering remains true after frame 2.
+- The same temporary path is then overwritten with valid GLB bytes from an existing fixture. A subsequent reload through the existing attached hook must succeed and produce frame 3.
+- Frame 3 must change the pixel signature relative to frames 1 and 2. An acceptable implementation is the existing `textured_uv_cube.glb` checkerboard assertion (`red_spread > 50 || blue_spread > 50`) plus a mismatch from the frame-1/frame-2 average RGB signature by more than `CUBE_THRESHOLD` on at least one channel.
+- No production behavior, watcher behavior, reload semantics, dependencies, fixtures, or docs are changed.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not change `EditorShell::handle_asset_reload`; this task only pins its existing failure-retention behavior from `rge-editor` tests.
+- Do not change the notify watcher, watcher tests, debounce timing, or redraw-drain integration. This issue is about the R-key/hook reload path.
+- Do not add tracing capture unless it is strictly test-local and requires no dependency or production changes. The required proof of parser failure is the hook returning `Err`.
+- Do not add a new fixture. Use temporary bytes for the malformed GLB and copy bytes from an existing valid fixture for the recovery reload.
+- Do not relax existing no-GPU skip behavior. If no headless adapter is available, direct malformed `Err` coverage should still run where practical, and the execution report must state which pixel assertions skipped.
+- Do not commit or push.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each applicable gate in their `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor --bin rge-editor r_key_reload_on_malformed -- --nocapture` -> expected exit 0. If the final test name differs, use the exact new test name and document it.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor --bin rge-editor r_key_reload -- --nocapture` -> expected exit 0, covering the new malformed regression alongside the existing R-key reload tests.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor --bin rge-editor` -> expected exit 0.
+- `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` -> expected exit 0, unless it fails for a clearly unrelated pre-existing prerequisite after all targeted gates above pass; if so, document the exact failure and halt.
+- `git status --short` -> expected changed files limited to `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` and the required `ISSUE-89_EXEC_*.md` execution report.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without committing and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- Implementing the regression appears to require changes to `EditorShell::handle_asset_reload`, watcher code, production reload code, GLB importer code, render code, dependencies, fixtures, or files outside the allowed scope.
+- The malformed parser-failure path cannot be asserted as `Err` without changing the hook or production API.
+- The valid recovery reload cannot be proven with existing fixtures and the existing hook path.
+- The frame-retention assertion cannot be expressed with the existing readback helpers and `CUBE_THRESHOLD` tolerance.
+- A new fixture, golden image, source file, dependency, or Cargo metadata change appears necessary.
+- Any required verification gate fails for a reason unrelated to this test-only change and cannot be resolved within the allowed scope.
+- The working tree contains unrelated edits in allowed files that would make a precise test-only diff ambiguous.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+The existing `r_key_reload_on_missing_file_preserves_prior_frame` test already establishes the intended shape: build a shell from `cube.glb`, render before reload, attach a `GlbLoaderHook`, call `shell.handle_asset_reload()`, then render again and compare the central-row signature. This dispatch should keep that structure but stage a real temporary file whose contents are wrong-magic bytes, so the failure is a parser error rather than an I/O missing-file error.
+
+For the recovery leg, reuse the same attached path and overwrite its bytes with an existing valid fixture such as `textured_uv_cube.glb`. That proves the failed parse did not poison the hook/source path and that a subsequent valid reload still reaches the normal successful swap path.
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-89
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+NEXT_ROLE: EXECUTOR_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---
